title: Water worlds have no surface slots
rule: Water Worlds do not provide surface construction slots, so surface facilities cannot be recommended on them.
planner_implications: Surface recommendations on Water Worlds must be blocked.

title: Construction points are distinct from material cost
rule: Construction-point gating and material cost should be modeled as separate planning dimensions.
planner_implications: A build can be blocked by CP even when commodities are available, or appear CP-negative in projection while the immediate next build is still valid.

title: Tier 1 facilities generate Tier 2 construction points
rule: Tier 1 facilities require no construction points and generate Tier 2 construction points.
planner_implications: Early sequencing should prioritize how T1 unlocks later options.

title: Tier 2 facilities consume Tier 2 and generate Tier 3 construction points
rule: Tier 2 facilities consume Tier 2 construction points and generate Tier 3 construction points.
planner_implications: Tier 2 sequencing determines which T3 options become available.

title: Tier 3 facilities consume Tier 3 construction points and do not generate more
rule: Tier 3 facilities consume Tier 3 construction points and are terminal in the basic generation chain.
planner_implications: T3 commitments should be planned carefully because they do not unlock further tiers.

title: The initial Primary Port is a construction-point exception
rule: The first Primary Port appears to generate relevant construction points without requiring construction points to be established.
planner_implications: Initial port creation should be modeled separately from later facility builds.
unknowns: Whether all initial-port classes behave identically

title: Current buildability differs from proposed full-plan totals
rule: A negative projected CP state across a full future plan is not enough to conclude that the next selected build is impossible right now.
planner_implications: Separate `current buildability` from `proposed build plan`.

title: Placeholder names and visible previews are not enough to infer completion state
rule: Placeholder names, proposal labels, or preview panels do not by themselves prove that a facility is completed and mechanically active.
planner_implications: Distinguish proposed, under-construction, previewed, and completed states.

title: The completed primary port is effectively permanent
rule: Once completed, the primary port cannot be changed; it can only be cancelled or abandoned before completion.
planner_implications: Primary-port selection needs stronger review than ordinary additive build decisions.

title: T2 and T3 port escalation begins when construction starts
rule: The escalating CP cost for Tier 2 and Tier 3 ports begins as soon as construction is initiated, not when the port is completed.
planner_implications: Starting a high-tier port early can make every later high-tier port more expensive immediately.

title: The primary port is exempt from high-tier port escalation
rule: The primary port does not count toward the escalating T2/T3 port cost limit.
planner_implications: A T3 primary port effectively grants a free top-tier anchor outside later CP escalation math.

title: T1 ports, settlements, hubs, and installations are exempt from high-tier port escalation
rule: T1 ports and all non-port facility families are exempt from the escalating T2/T3 port cost mechanic.
planner_implications: Use exempt facility families to expand support without inflating later T2/T3 port costs.

title: Construction concurrency is capped at five facilities
rule: No more than five facilities can be under construction at the same time in one system.
planner_implications: Build sequencing must be scheduled in waves of five or fewer.

title: Large settlements are the best green-CP conversion tool
rule: Large Tier 2 settlements convert one yellow CP into two green CP, making them uniquely efficient green-CP generators.
planner_implications: Use large settlements for temporary or permanent green-CP farming in capital builds.
testing_status: Extracted from Mega Guide v2.3.0 and DaftMav-derived tables

title: Primary-installation material surcharges are tier-specific
rule: The first installation carries a resource surcharge of +16% for Tier 1, +32% for Tier 2, and +20% for Tier 3.
planner_implications: Add primary-installation surcharges into first-wave logistics instead of using standard facility totals.

title: Medium settlements cost exactly double small settlements of the same type
rule: Medium settlements require exactly 2x the resources of small settlements of the same type.
planner_implications: Resource planning for medium settlements can be safely estimated from small-settlement baselines.

title: Large settlements cost exactly triple small settlements of the same type
rule: Large settlements require exactly 3x the resources of small settlements of the same type.
planner_implications: Large-settlement hauling estimates can be derived directly from small-settlement baselines.

title: Military Installations require a Military Settlement
rule: Military Installations require a Military Settlement as a prerequisite.
planner_implications: Military space infrastructure is a two-step chain, not a direct pick.

title: Security Stations require a Relay Station
rule: Security Stations require a Relay Station as a prerequisite.
planner_implications: Security-focused orbital chains need an earlier communications step.

title: Research Stations require a Bio Research Settlement
rule: Research Stations require a Bio Research Settlement as a prerequisite.
planner_implications: High Tech orbital research routes need matching ground science support.

title: Tourist Installations require a Tourism Settlement
rule: Tourist Installations require a Tourism Settlement as a prerequisite.
planner_implications: Tourism orbital chains start with a ground-tourism dependency.

title: Tourism settlements require a Satellite Installation
rule: Tourism settlements require a Satellite Installation as a prerequisite.
planner_implications: Tourism chains consume both an orbital support slot and a ground slot before hubs or higher tourism effects.

title: Multiple hub classes have facility-specific prerequisites
rule: Surface hubs do not share a universal prerequisite; each hub class uses a specific settlement or installation requirement.
planner_implications: Hub planning must be dependency-aware at the individual facility class level.
